role: A per-panel React error boundary that isolates malformed or throwing panel definitions so the surrounding canvas remains usable.
capabilities: Catches render errors in child panels via getDerivedStateFromError
Displays a contained fallback card naming the failed panel
Prevents a full-canvas white-screen for operator control surface
substance_why: It fully implements the error-boundary lifecycle with state, fallback rendering, and an informative message, despite being small.
interactions_present: None — it only renders a static failure message.
interactions_missing: No error reset or retry mechanism in the UI
No action to revert/remove the failed panel (recovery is only described as bridge-side)
No error details, stack trace, or reporting
No dismiss/close button for the error card
performance_risks: None significant; the component is simple and only re-renders on error state change.
reuse_verdict_why: The containment concept is valuable, but it should be modernized to a functional component with error reset, better fallback UI, and optional error logging.
decomposition_hints: Split the fallback UI into its own presentational component
Separate error-handling logic from the displayed error card
Add a reset/error-info seam for integration with logging/telemetry
